rows.snippet: Using Chrome or Edge 136 and higher with Laserfiche Connector may result in an error stating the browser is not configured correctly 1 year ago 136 and higher with Laserfiche Connector may result in an error stating the browser is not configured correctly May 9, 2025 | KB: 1014571 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014571/using ... 136... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014571/using-chrome-or-edge-136-and-high ...
rows.title: Using Chrome or Edge 136 and higher with Laserfiche Connector may result in an error stating the browser is not configured correctly
rows.detailTitle: Using Chrome or Edge 136 and higher with Laserfiche Connector may result in an error stating the browser is not configured correctly
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Using Chrome or Edge 136 and higher with Laserfiche Connector may result in an error stating the browser is not configured correctly Product: Laserfiche Connector KB: 1014571 Published: May 1, 2025 Last Updated: May 9, 2025 Summary When trying to use an existing Laserfiche Connector profile or when trying to configure a new Laserfiche Connector profile with Chrome or Edge 136 or higher, users may see an error stating: Chrome is not configured correctly. Follow these instructions to configure Chrome. New Microsoft Edge is not configured correctly. Follow these instructions to configure new Microsoft Edge. Cause Chrome and Edge 136 and higher now require the use of an additional switch, --user-data-dir, to be used in conjunction with --remote-debugging-port. See this post for additional details. Resolution Modify the Chrome shortcut target and after --remote-debugging-port=9222 --remote-allow-origins=localhost append the --user-data-dir switch. It should now resemble --remote-debugging-port=9222 --remote-allow-origins=localhost --user-data-dir=C:\MyCustomPath Modify the Edge shortcut target and after --remote-debugging-port=9322 --remote-allow-origins=localhost append the --user-data-dir switch. It should now resemble --remote-debugging-port=9322 --remote-allow-origins=localhost --user-data-dir=C:\MyCustomPath Close and reopen Chrome or Edge and Laserfiche Connector after making and saving the change. The user data directory contains profile data such as history, bookmarks, cookies, etc. Specifying a custom path with the --user-data-dir flag will override the default location of the user data directory and create a new profile, meaning the launched instance of Chrome or Edge will not have any of the settings previously configured for the default profile. Depending on your preferences, you may want to transfer settings over to the custom profile or have two different browser shortcuts, one for use with Laserfiche Connector and another for other web browsing needs. More Information See Google documentation discussing the change to the remote debugging switches. https://developer.chrome.com/blog/remote-debugging-port Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

Error Messages: Take note of any error messages that occurred. Please be precise in getting the exact text of the ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014509/general-technical-support-checkli ...
rows.title: General Technical Support Checklist for Laserfiche Cloud
rows.detailTitle: General Technical Support Checklist for Laserfiche Cloud
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith General Technical Support Checklist for Laserfiche Cloud Product: Laserfiche Cloud KB: 1014509 Published: March 26, 2024 Last Updated: April 19, 2024 Summary There are certain pieces of information that are regularly requested for most support cases, regardless of the specific Laserfiche product you are using. Having this information ready can expedite the handling of your support case. For solution providers opening a case from the support site, please have this information beforehand and provide the information when you open the case. Commonly requested information and files Provide the Cloud Account ID If necessary, grant Support Engineers temporary access to the account. Error Messages: Take note of any error messages that occurred. Please be precise in getting the exact text of the error message. Screenshots and timestamps will be very useful. How to reproduce the error: Provide either screenshots or a recorded video of the steps you took leading up to the error. If there are multiple screenshots, please insert the images into a single Microsoft Word document for ease of transmission. If providing a video, please upload a standard video file format or a hyperlink to a commonly-accepted video hosting platform. Files needed to reproduce the error: Please provide sample material necessary to reproduce the issue. For example: workflow, business process, sample documents, or a briefcase of repository objects. Scale and scope: Please note whether the problem is specific to one Cloud Account ID, type of web browser, user, group, template, repository, document, page, etc. If the issue is more widespread, take note whether it occurs in all situations. Changes made prior: Provide a description of any changes to the system or program that were made prior to the error occurring. Web Client Issue: Modify the tracing level for the user experiencing the issue. From the app picker, select the repository. Select the username and choose Options. On the Advanced page, change the Tracing setting from Critical to Debug and click Start. Provide Laserfiche Support with the Reference ID. Recreate the issue in question. Stop tracing after recreating the issue. Business Process Issue: If the issue is related to an instance that is in-progress, terminated, or suspended, take note of the specific Business Process name, instance IDs, the exact time an error occurred, and the specific step that the error occurred in. Take screenshots of the History tab and copy and paste the text of any error including the full stack trace shown from clicking View error log on the problematic step. Provide Laserfiche Support a copy of the Business Process (XML) along with any associated components, e.g. workflow, business rule, etc. Workflow Issue: If the issue is related to the running of a workflow instance, take note of the specific instance details such as workflow name, instance name, the exact time an error occurred, and the specific activity that the error occurred with. Take screenshots of all tabs of the workflow instance details. Provide Laserfiche Support a copy of the workflow (WFI). Import Agent Issue: On the computer hosting Import Agent, open the Windows Event Viewer and save .EVTX or .EVT copies of the Adminand Operational logs for Import Agent within the Applications and Services Logs section. Remote Agent Issue: Provide the version of remote agent worker. Save copies of Admin and Operational logs from > Event Viewer > Applications and Service Logs > Laserfiche > ProcessAutomation > LocalAgent . Export the contents of the entire folder from C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\BPMServer Windows Client Issue: Provide the version of the Windows client. Enable trace logs from Windows Client > Help > About Laserfiche and click on Tracing Enable LFSO tracing: This option will log a majority of the client's communication with the repository Enable DocumentProcessing tracing: This option logs certain actions involving client-side document processing Enable Office Integration tracing: this option logs actions involving local editing of Office documents stored in the repository Turning on tracing creates text log files written to the location specified in the dialog at the time tracing was enabled. Be sure to turn off tracing once you are done testing. Scanning Issue: Provide the scanning service ( C:\Program Files (x86)\Laserfiche\Scanning Service\LaserficheScanning.Service.exe ) and webtools agent (C:\Program Files (x86)\Laserfiche\Webtools Agent\Laserfiche Webtools Agent.exe ) file versions. Please refer to Laserfiche Scanning 10 Technical Support Checklist for the complete list. Quick Fields Issue: Provide the Quick Fields version, a copy of the .qfx file for the Quick Fields session that is encountering problems, along with a briefcase containing the template/fields used in the session. Please refer to Quick Fields 10 Technical Support Checklist for the complete list. Important Make sure that you install the late

rows.snippet: Release Notes for Connector 11 4 weeks ago You may receive a "there is no document currently open" error when using the "Start workflow" or "Start Workflow business process" actions with the "Currently... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014248/release-notes-for-connector-11
rows.title: Release Notes for Connector 11
rows.detailTitle: Release Notes for Connector 11
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Release Notes for Connector 11 Product: Laserfiche Connector KB: 1014248 Published: March 5, 2021 Last Updated: June 2, 2026 Summary Laserfiche Connector provides a streamlined experience for integrating Laserfiche with line of business applications such as Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems. Laserfiche Connector 11 is a major release that includes important updates for user authentication, capture via UI automation, date formatting, and more. See Knowledge Base article 1014247 for a list of changes in Laserfiche Connector 11. Service Pack and Hotfix Information 1014585 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector Update 3 1014489 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector Update 2 1014379 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector Update 1 Requirements System Requirements CPU: 2.93GHz or faster processor Memory: 4GB Operating system: Windows 7, Windows 10, Windows Server 2012 or later Software Requirements .NET 4.8 Microsoft Edge WebView2 Laserfiche WebTools Agent Microsoft Visual C++ 2015-2019 Redistributable (x86) -14.28.29325 Microsoft Visual C++ 2015-2019 Redistributable (x64) -14.28.29325 Laserfiche Server version 10 or later Laserfiche Web Client version 10 or later Laserfiche WebLink 9.0 or later Laserfiche Scanning 10 or later Laserfiche Workflow 10 or later Laserfiche Directory Server 10.4.5 or later To integrate Laserfiche Connector with a web application Internet Explorer: versions 9, 10, or 11 Firefox: the latest version (auto-updated) Chrome: the latest version (auto-updated) Microsoft Edge based on Chromium: the latest version (auto-updated) To integrate Laserfiche Connector with a Java Application on Windows system Java Runtime Environment (JRE): versions 6 update 21, or later Java Development Kit (JDK): versions 6 update 21, or later Java Access Bridge (JAB): version 2.0.2 or later enabled Known Issues Connector 11 does not support writing data to Microsoft Word, Excel, and Access 2019. (597653) The "Laserfiche Cloud" and "Laserfiche Directory Server authentication" options are not available when creating a profile that scans documents in to the Laserfiche Windows client. (299268) When configuring a search repository profile that search the Laserfiche Windows client, the Use TLS connection checkbox is not available. To learn how to configure Laserfiche Connector to use TLS, see the online help files. (30696) You may receive a "there is no document currently open" error when using the "Start workflow" or "Start Workflow business process" actions with the "Currently open Laserfiche document" option if Connector is launched as an administrator, but the Laserfiche client is not. (44788) Connector 11 does not support editing Connector 10.2 profiles that use the "Write data to an application" action. (52459) When using Laserfiche Connector on a remote desktop, and when using OCR to analyze a window, you may get unexpected results if the target application is partially hidden by another application. Ensure the application you are targeting does not have any windows or applications showing in front of it. (128129) If the control you are getting information from has any of the following characters in its ID: " !"#$%&'()*+,./:;<=>?@[\]^`{|}~", the profile will not run correctly. Escape these characters by adding \\ (two backslashes) before them in the profile's XML. (30690, 153713, 146019) A profile run from the command line will silently fail if Laserfiche Connector is run as an administrator and the command is not run as an administrator. (50636) The %(Count) token is not resolved when a Laserfiche Connector profile runs. (140853) When selecting a control in Chrome with UI automation, you cannot select controls hidden behind a scroll bar inside an iFrame that has a different source. (120818) If you change text size through Personalize -> Display in Windows, Laserfiche Connector selection rectangles may be offset in certain applications. To get the rectangles to be position correctly, open the properties for the target application and select "Disable display scaling on high DPI settings" on the Compatibility tab. After you've configured Laserfiche Connector, you can clear this option. (127127) When selecting an application with the Profile Creator, you cannot select a browser window when the browser is in full screen mode. (117931) When a Firefox browser is in full screen mode, clicking the overlay button will make the action list disappear. (117566) If you take a screenshot of a web page that is zoomed in or zoomed out, the quality may suffer. Screenshot pages at 100% zoom level. (147987) When selecting a control in Chrome with UI automation, Chrome will crash if you select an application on a web page that has a multi-selection element. (121613) If a profile is configured to prompt for login information when scanning in the Laserfiche Windows client, and the user enters Windows account credentials instead of Laserfiche accoun

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 1 Product: Laserfiche Connector KB: 1014379 Published: June 7, 2022 Last Updated: June 2, 2026 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 1. This update has been superseded by KB 1014585 Laserfiche Connector Update 3. Fixes for Laserfiche Connector The Laserfiche Connector test utility can now interact with Firefox browser tabs. (361639) Laserfiche Connector profiles now appear for Microsoft Edge version 101. (379018) "Valid flow is not found" errors no longer occur when testing an existing profile using command line parameters. (202046) When running a profile that interacts with the Laserfiche web client, you can now use the "domain\username" format when prompted to sign into the Laserfiche web client. (228596) The update includes the following file: LFC.exe (11.0.0.200) Resolution To update Laserfiche Connector: Click the following link to download the update: KB1014379.zip. On the computer hosting Laserfiche Connector, extract and run LFConnector_1100200_KB1014379_Update1.exe. Related Links List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 3 1014248 Release Notes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: Error - There is a problem with Adobe/Acrobat Reader. If it is running, please exit and try again. (523:523)
rows.detailTitle: Error - There is a problem with Adobe/Acrobat Reader. If it is running, please exit and try again. (523:523)
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Error - There is a problem with Adobe/Acrobat Reader. If it is running, please exit and try again. (523:523) Product: Laserfiche 11 KB: 1014516 Published: March 12, 2024 Last Updated: March 26, 2024 Summary When trying to export a PDF within Laserfiche, you may receive the following error: There is a problem with Adobe/Acrobat Reader. If it is running, please exit and try again. (523:523) Possible Causes Protected mode may be enabled in Acrobat. Resolution Disable Protected mode in Acrobat by clearing the Enable Protected Mode selection in startup in the Security (Enhanced) dialog:. See Adobe's community discussion page on the error for detailed instructions. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 3 Product: Laserfiche Connector KB: 1014585 Published: June 2, 2026 Last Updated: June 2, 2026 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 update 3. This update supersedes Laserfiche Connector 11 update 2. Fixes Fixed an issue where Laserfiche Scanning profiles launched from Laserfiche Connector did not open correctly when Laserfiche Server was configured to use a custom port. (504213) Fixed an issue where special characters in field values were not correctly escaped when scanning documents or pages into the Laserfiche Repository Web Client. (620971) Fixed an issue where Laserfiche Connector could not retrieve website URLs from Firefox 149.x and 150.x browser windows. (658396) Various security improvements. (504697, 511973, 511974, 511975, 517233, 517234) Improved French translation. (600828) Updated the wording for error message 20097. (656797) To update Laserfiche Connector Click the following link to download the update: KB1014585.zip On the computer hosting Laserfiche Connector, extract and run LFConnector_110260570_KB1014585_Update3.exe Related Links List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 2 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 1 Release Notes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 2
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 2
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 2 Product: Laserfiche Connector KB: 1014489 Published: November 13, 2023 Last Updated: June 2, 2026 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 update 2. This update has been superseded by KB 1014585 Laserfiche Connector Update 3. Fixes When scanning via the web client, token values now properly resolve for metadata fields that contain the # character. (407345) In the Laserfiche Connector Profile Creation Wizard, adding a token from the token dialog no longer errors with "Object reference not set to an instance of an object." (482926) Credentials saved in a previous version of Laserfiche Connector are remembered and retained when Laserfiche Connector is upgraded. (483087) Token values can now be retrieved when using Laserfiche Connector with Chrome version 111.0.5563.65 and higher. (417750) The Laserfiche Connector help files now clarify that the remote debugging port is 9322 for Microsoft Edge, not 9222 as specified by Microsoft, which ensures that Edge integrations do not conflict with Chrome integrations. (426216) The "Learn more" link in the Laserfiche Connector Profile Creation Wizard and Configuration dialog now correctly links to the Laserfiche Connector help files. (482844) To update Laserfiche Connector Click the following link to download the update: 1014489.zip On the computer hosting Laserfiche Connector, extract and run LFConnector_1100226_KB1014489_Update2.exe Related Links List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 3 List of Changes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Update 1 Release Notes for Laserfiche Connector 11 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "Error reading XML file - Name cannot begin with the ';' character” Error Occurs When Attempting to Upload Process 3 years ago A "Error reading XML file - Name cannot begin with the ';' character” Error Occurs When Attempting to Upload Process February 8, 2023 | KB: 1014420 https://support.laserfich... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014420/a-error-reading-xml-file-n ...
rows.title: A "Error reading XML file - Name cannot begin with the ';' character” Error Occurs When Attempting to Upload Process
rows.detailTitle: A "Error reading XML file - Name cannot begin with the ';' character” Error Occurs When Attempting to Upload Process
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "Error reading XML file - Name cannot begin with the ';' character” Error Occurs When Attempting to Upload Process Product: Forms KB: 1014420 Published: February 8, 2023 Last Updated: February 8, 2023 Summary When attempting to upload process with previously downloaded process XML file, you may receive following error: Error reading XML file - Name cannot begin with the ';' character, hexadecimal value 0x3B... [LFF317-UnableToReadXml] Cause New Relic .NET Agent injects the process XML with "<script>" block when download the process if there is New Relic .NET Agent installed on the Forms Server. Workaround If you still want to use the existing downloaded process XML file to upload process, you can edit the XML file and remove the "<script>" blocks inside "<suspendNotification>" and "<terminalNotification>" blocks. You can also disable instrumentation for FormsAppPool from New Relic .NET Agent configuration so it will not inject the process XML files any more when download process using following steps: 1. Go to C:\ProgramData\New Relic\.NET Agent on the Forms Server 2. Open newrelic.config 3. Add the following code under configuration node: <applicationPools> <applicationPool name="FormsAppPool" instrument="false"/> </applicationPools>" 4. Restart IIS Related Links New Relic .NET agent configuration documentation Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Laserfiche Directory Server fails to validate email addresses from some valid top-level domains 1 year ago Laserfiche Directory Server fails to validate email addresses from some valid top-level domains March 31, 2025 | KB: 1014566 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014566/laserfiche-directory-serve... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014566/laserfiche-directory-server-fa ...
rows.title: Laserfiche Directory Server fails to validate email addresses from some valid top-level domains
rows.detailTitle: Laserfiche Directory Server fails to validate email addresses from some valid top-level domains
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Laserfiche Directory Server fails to validate email addresses from some valid top-level domains Product: Directory Server 11, Directory Server 12 KB: 1014566 Published: March 24, 2025 Last Updated: March 31, 2025 Summary When attempting to add a user in Laserfiche Directory Server, the application fails to validate the email addresses from some valid top-level domains Cause Directory Server has a default list of allowed top-level domains. Workaround Backup the original C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Directory Server\Web\LFDS\Release\Scripts\app.min.js file to a new folder Edit the C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Directory Server\Web\LFDS\Release\Scripts\app.min.js file. Look for the list of allowed email domains that includes the following like: local|mil and change that to local|mil|add_additional_domain_here Save the changes and clear the browser cache Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: PDF generation fails when using Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge for PuppeteerExecutablePath in Forms 11 Update 2 or later 1 year ago PDF generation fails when using Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge for PuppeteerExecutablePath in Forms 11 Update 2 or later January 30, 2025 | KB: 1014543 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/101454... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014543/pdf-generation-fails-when- ...
rows.title: PDF generation fails when using Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge for PuppeteerExecutablePath in Forms 11 Update 2 or later
rows.detailTitle: PDF generation fails when using Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge for PuppeteerExecutablePath in Forms 11 Update 2 or later
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith PDF generation fails when using Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge for PuppeteerExecutablePath in Forms 11 Update 2 or later Product: Laserfiche Forms 11, Laserfiche Forms 12 KB: 1014543 Published: August 29, 2024 Last Updated: January 30, 2025 Summary Update on January 28, 2025: Chrome and Edge version 132 includes a change that removes the need for a previous Forms 11 Update 5 hotfix included in this article. Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 with Hotfix 1014514 (11.0.2311.50564) no longer encounters the issue previous documented in this article. The hotfix has been removed from this Knowledge Base article. If you have previously manually applied the Laserfiche Forms 11 hotfix from this article, see the instructions below to revert the change. For Laserfiche Forms 12, see the instructions below to insert an option to update the behavior. What to do if you applied the hotfix previously available for Forms 11 If you manually replaced FormsCommonUtils.dll to version 11.0.2311.50585, revert FormsCommonUtils.dll to version 11.0.2311.50564 by uninstalling and reinstalling Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 with Hotfix 1014514. Open the Windows Control Panel, click Programs, and click View installed updates. Locate Laserfiche Forms x64 11.0.2311.50564 Hotfix 1 KB:1014514 and click Uninstall. reinstall Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 with Hotfix 1014514 to revert to FormsCommonUtils.dll version 11.0.2311.50564 What to do if you are on Laserfiche Forms 12 For Laserfiche Forms 12, Insert a new row in the cf_options table to change how Laserfiche Forms invokes Chrome or Edge. See the following sample SQL script to insert the PuppeteerHeadlessMode option to the Forms database's cf_options table and set the option value to 0: INSERT INTO [dbo].[cf_options] ([optionname],[optionvalue]) VALUES ('PuppeteerHeadlessMode', 0); Restart the Laserfiche Forms Routing Service. Related Links List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5. List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 Hotfix 1014514. Using Either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge for PDF Generation with Forms 11 Update 2. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailTitle: Release Notes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Product: Workflow, Forms KB: 1014522 Published: May 17, 2024 Last Updated: May 25, 2026 Summary The Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool analyzes your workflows and business processes to determine which items can be automatically migrated to Laserfiche Cloud and which need manual configuration either before or after migration. 1014523 List of Features for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool. Service Pack and Hotfix Information See the most current service pack and hotfix information in the Laserfiche 12 changelog. 1014584 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2605 1014579 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2510 1014576 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2507 1014573 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2505 1014549 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2411 1014542 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2409 1014540 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2407 1014528 List of Changes for the Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Update 1.0.2406 System Requirements Supported Operating Systems Windows 10 or later, 64-bit version only Windows Server 2012 or later, 64-bit version only Note: Linux is not currently supported. On Windows Server 2012 and Windows Server 2012 R2, you must install Universal C Runtime first. Requirements for the Assessment Tool Minimum Requirements CPU: 1GHz Memory: 1GB RAM Recommended Requirements CPU: 2.6GHz or faster processor Memory: 2GB RAM or higher Software Requirements Supported Web Browsers Google Chrome: latest version (auto-updated) Microsoft Edge: latest version (auto-updated) Firefox: latest version (auto-updated) Supported Laserfiche Products Laserfiche Forms: 10.4 or later Laserfiche Workflow: 10.4 or later Supported Source Database Engines SQL Server 2008 SQL Server 2008R2 SQL Server 2012 SQL Server 2014 SQL Server 2016 SQL Server 2017 SQL Server 2019 SQL Server 2022 Note: Oracle databases are not currently supported. General Information The Assessment Tool requires Windows Authentication to sign in. Different users on a single machine can run separate Assessment Tools at the same time. Users can only access their own Assessment Tool and cannot access others' tools on the same machine. The Assessment Tool automatically checks for the latest version and automatically offers upgrades, as long as the tool has network access. The Assessment Tool is only currently provided in English. Installation Information You can launch the Assessment Tool without installing it. When starting Assessment Tool, the tool will open in a web browser and a background process will be created. You can close the web page at any time and running assessments will complete in the background. You can launch Assessment Tool again to reopen the web page. Known Issues The Assessment Tool only supports Laserfiche Forms 10.4 and later. (516047, 516053, 516055, 516064) Rarely, the assessment status stall in the "waiting" status. Force quit the Assessment Tool process and restart the tool. Then, delete the failed assessment history and click Reassess. (520579) Rarely, the assessment may fail with a critical database error. Delete the failed assessment and create a new one. (521559) Sometimes the "Reassess" button appears disabled even though the report has finished. Refresh the page to enable the button. (520586) The dialog box that appears when updating the Assessment Tool to the latest version only shows the relevant Release Note and List of Changes KB numbers. It does not provide links to the KBs. Related Links 1014523 List of Features for Laserfiche Cloud Migration Readiness Assessment Tool Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Windows Authentication fails after upgrading Directory Server 4 years ago Windows Authentication fails after upgrading Directory Server May 5, 2022 | KB: 1014262 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014262/windows-authentication-fails-after-upgrading-directory-s... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014262/windows-authentication-fails
rows.title: Windows Authentication fails after upgrading Directory Server
rows.detailTitle: Windows Authentication fails after upgrading Directory Server
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Windows Authentication fails after upgrading Directory Server Product: Directory Server KB: 1014262 Published: March 18, 2021 Last Updated: May 5, 2022 Summary Windows Authentication in Directory Server fails on Microsoft Edge and possibly, other internet browsers when: Upgrading to a newer version of Directory Server Before upgrade, the Directory Server or STS IIS application pool identities were changed to a value other than the default value of NetworkService Cause When upgrading to a newer version of Directory Server, any changes made to the IIS application pool identities for Directory Server or STS are overwritten with the default value of NetworkService. This may cause Windows Authentication to fail or behave inconsistently across internet browsers. Resolution This issue is no longer relevant when upgrading to Laserfiche Directory Server 11 Update 2 or later. You can specify application pool identities during the installation process. Workaround Prior to upgrading Directory Server note the IIS application pool identities. After upgrade is complete, manually change the IIS application pool identities to what they were prior to the upgrade. Note: Directory Server application pool is named LicenseManagerAppPool. STS application pool is named LicenseManagerSTSAppPool. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: Release Notes for Directory Server 11 Update 6
rows.detailTitle: Release Notes for Directory Server 11 Update 6
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Directory Server 11 Update 6 Product: Directory Server 11 KB: 1014512 Published: April 1, 2024 Last Updated: April 1, 2024 Summary See Knowledge Base article 1014511 for a list of changes in Directory Sever 11 Update 6. Laserfiche Directory Server 11 controls licensing and activation for Laserfiche products in Laserfiche Rio, Laserfiche Avante, and subscription-based systems. An administrator can generate application licenses for available Laserfiche applications, can allocate available licenses to user accounts, and perform other actions involving license administration. Laserfiche Directory Server 11 can also provide user authentication for Laserfiche 10+ products. This allows single sign-on across the Laserfiche web client, Laserfiche Forms, Laserfiche App, and Laserfiche Discussions. For example, a user can sign in to the web client and be automatically signed in when viewing Laserfiche Forms. Directory Server administration is available through an administration website. Service Pack and Hotfix Information There are no current service packs or hotfixes. Upgrade Information Directory Server 11 can be installed over existing Directory Server 10, Directory Server 9, and License Manager 8.3.1 installations. When creating a Directory Server licensing site, you have the option of importing data from a License Manager 8 database into the new Directory Server licensing site. The import process will migrate registered application information, named users license assignments, and version 8.3.x Active Directory group synchronization rules into the Directory Server site. Directory Server 11 requires Laserfiche Server version 10.2.1 and later or Laserfiche Server 10.1 Update 3 (KB 1013882). Upgrading Directory Server from versions prior to 10.4.3 requires changes to STS configuration (KB 1014134). Requirements Minimum System Requirements CPU: Dual-core processor Memory: 2 GB RAM Supported 64-bit Operating Systems Laserfiche Directory Server is only supported on 64-bit versions of Microsoft Windows. Windows 10 Windows 11 Windows Server 2016 Windows Server 2019 Windows Server 2022 Microsoft Windows Components .NET Framework 4.8 IIS 10 or later HTTPS Supported Versions of Microsoft SQL Server Microsoft SQL Server 2014 (Service Pack 3) Microsoft SQL Server 2016 Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Microsoft SQL Server 2019 Microsoft SQL Server 2022 Supported Browsers for the Web Administration Console Microsoft Edge (latest version) Firefox (latest version) Chrome (latest version) Known Issues The login of AD provider's Window account is affected by LDAP provider's setting. (511863) Cannot disable Windows Authentication on original AD Identity Provider. (499541) Prior to Directory Server 11 Update 5, event data for the "Identity Provider - Set General Config" event type always displayed "Enable Windows Authentication" as Yes. (229183) Prior to Directory Server 11 Update 5, event data for the "Identity Provider - Set General Config" event type displayed the values for Name/Host/Use TLS/Enable Active Directory synchronization prior to the modification. (229183) Directory Server may not log an event for certain situations involving long user names. (486263) If you add two STS sites with the same single logout Directory Server endpoints, the auto-generated service provider metadata file may contain duplicate items. (484758) Directory Server may not log an event when removing linked identity providers. (484414) Directory Server may sign you out of all your sessions when processing a single logout request when you are signed in with multiple different user accounts in separate browser tabs. (484214) If a batch import with a CSV initially fails, you may need to refresh the page to successfully import a new CSV. (484169) When using a non-English language, the "Yes/No" label in various "Yes/No" user interface elements may be cut off. (483992) The Azure sign-in page may become unresponsive after clicking the browser back button. (483943) Due to the implementation changes to support SCIM2, if you have custom code using License Manager Objects (LMO) that calls the SetScimSettings or GetScimSettings methods, you must update to the current LMO library. The installation wizard may not automatically fill in the existing password for the Directory Server service user when upgrading from a previous installation. (424577) Directory Server may log excess "Access denied" error messages when non-administrators browse the Directory Server website. (343151) There is no audit event for when the "Decrypt SAML assertions" option is turned on or off. (306482) When Active Directory Synchronization is enabled with an interval poll value of 0 or 1, the polling runs every 90 minutes. (302907) When editing certain registered application instances, the "Assign application license to" option may not display the previously selected value. (302434) Creating an organization right after deleting an organization resul

rows.detailTitle: A "Retrieving the COM class factory for component with CLSID {D94D3976-B933-495E-AD3E-9D6FA6CB418D} failed due to the following error: 80080005" Error Occurs In Laserfiche Scanning 9.1.1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "Retrieving the COM class factory for component with CLSID {D94D3976-B933-495E-AD3E-9D6FA6CB418D} failed due to the following error: 80080005" Error Occurs In Laserfiche Scanning 9.1.1. Product: Scanning 9.1.1 KB: 1013569 Published: November 3, 2014 Last Updated: April 28, 2022 Summary After installing Laserfiche 9.1.1 Service Pack 1 on a 64-bit version of Microsoft Windows, Laserfiche Scanning may display the following error message: Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation. Retrieving the COM class factory for component with CLSID {D94D3976-B933-495E-AD3E-9D6FA6CB418D} failed due to the following error: 80080005 Cause The upgrade process may incorrectly update certain 32-bit OCR components with the 64-bit version files. Workaround Manually replace the affected DLL files with the 32-bit versions. Download the following ZIP file containing the 32-bit replacement files. KB1013569 Extract the contents of the ZIP file to the following locations: C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\9.1\BPImageWrapper91.dll C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\Batch Processor\BPInterfacesClient.dll C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\Batch Processor\BPObjectsClient91.dll C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\Batch Processor\BPOCRClientWrapper90.dll C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\Batch Processor\BPPageFixClient91.dll C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Laserfiche\Batch Processor\Laserfiche.BatchProcessor.Utils.dll Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Windows Authentication fails due to an incorrect automatic setting in the UserSettings.config file 5 years ago Windows Authentication fails due to an incorrect automatic setting in the UserSettings.config file November 30, 2020 | KB: 1014252 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014252/windows-authenticati... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014252/windows-authentication-fails
rows.title: Windows Authentication fails due to an incorrect automatic setting in the UserSettings.config file
rows.detailTitle: Windows Authentication fails due to an incorrect automatic setting in the UserSettings.config file
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Windows Authentication fails due to an incorrect automatic setting in the UserSettings.config file Product: Directory Server KB: 1014252 Published: November 10, 2020 Last Updated: November 30, 2020 Summary Windows Authentication in Directory Server fails, resulting in administrators seeing one of the following errors: You do not have enough rights to view this site. Exception of type 'Laserfiche.LicenseManager.InvalidWindowsAuthenticationException' was thrown. (LMO122) Cause When visiting Directory Server, Windows Authentication fails due to an incorrect automatic host name setting in the UserSettings.config file. Resolution In the UserSettings.config file, replace all instances of the Directory Server machine's host name alias with the true host name of the machine. Note: By default, the UserSettings.config file is located in C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\LFDS. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailTitle: Error - 1064: An exception occurred in the service when handling the control request.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Error - 1064: An exception occurred in the service when handling the control request. Product: Directory Server 10, Directory Server 11 KB: 1014515 Published: March 12, 2024 Last Updated: March 26, 2024 Summary The Laserfiche Directory Server service will not start or continuously shuts down. Possible Causes The service account configured for Laserfiche Directory Server does not have sufficient rights. Resolution Grant sufficient rights to the Laserfiche service account. See Configuring the Directory Server Service User and Ports for detailed instructions. Use the Windows event log viewer for additional details. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: An "Exception of type 'System.Exception' was thrown." Error Occurs When Opening an Electronic Document In WebLink 8.
rows.detailTitle: An "Exception of type 'System.Exception' was thrown." Error Occurs When Opening an Electronic Document In WebLink 8.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Exception of type 'System.Exception' was thrown." Error Occurs When Opening an Electronic Document In WebLink 8. Product: WebLink 8 KB: 1013473 Published: April 21, 2014 Last Updated: April 21, 2014 Summary When you configure WebLink to open electronic documents using the The associated application option in WebLink 8, you may receive the following error when opening an electronic document on the WebLink Server: Exception of type ''System.Exception'' was thrown. When opening an electronic document with the associated application from a workstation, you may receive the following error message: 404 - File or directory not found. Cause This issue can occur if the user viewing the document does not have the Export feature right. Workaround Use the Laserfiche Administration Console or Laserfiche Web Administration Console to grant the appropriate WebLink user account the Export feature right. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: License File Locations.
rows.detailTitle: License File Locations.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith License File Locations. Product: Laserfiche 7, Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9, Laserfiche 10, Laserfiche 11, Laserfiche 12 KB: 1000988 Published: June 10, 2005 Last Updated: October 21, 2025 Summary This article documents license file locations for several Laserfiche products. More Information License files are usually placed in a product's root installation path with a few exceptions. The following contains a listing of different Laserfiche products and their license file locations (based on the default installation path). Currently supported products (version 10.4 or higher): Product License File Location Laserfiche App server C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Mobile Laserfiche Connector C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\LFConnector Forms C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\Forms\License Server C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Server Laserfiche Web client C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access Laserfiche Distributed Computing Cluster C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Distributed Computing Cluster Import Agent 10.4-11 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Import Agent Import Agent 12 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Import Agent Suite\Import Agent Email Archive 10.4-11 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Import Agent Email Archive 12 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Import Agent Suite\Import Agent Quick Fields C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\Quick Fields\Licenses Quick Fields Server C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\Quick Fields\Licenses\QFS WebLink C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink Legacy products: Version 10 Product License File Location Laserfiche App server C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Mobile Laserfiche Connector C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\LFConnector Laserfiche Distributed Computing Cluster C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Distributed Computing Cluster Discussions 10.1 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Discussions Forms 10-10.3 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Forms\Forms\bin Forms 10.4 C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche Forms\License Import Agent C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Import Agent Quick Fields C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\Quick Fields\Licenses Quick Fields Server C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\Quick Fields\Licenses\QFS Server C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Server Laserfiche Web client C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access WebLink C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink Version 9 Product License File Location Agenda Manager C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Agenda Manager Server Client C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Client Laserfiche Connector C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\LFConnector Forms C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Forms\Forms\bin Import Agent C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Import Agent Mobile server C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Mobile Quick Fields C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\Quick Fields\Licenses Server C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Server Social BPM C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Social BPM Web Access C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access WebLink C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink Version 8 Product License File Location Agenda Manager 8 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Agenda Manager Server Client 8 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Client 8 Import Agent 8 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Import Agent 8 Workflow 8 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Workflow Plus C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Plus 8.1 Quick Fields 8 C:\ProgramData\Laserfiche\Quick Fields\Licenses Server 8 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Server Web Access 8 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access 8 WebLink 8 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink 8 Version 7 Product License File Location Agenda Manager 7 C:\Program Files\Common Files\Laserfiche\Agenda Manager Import Agent 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Import Agent. Laserfiche Client 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Client Laserfiche Scanning C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Scanning Laserfiche Server 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Server Quick Fields 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Quick Fields Snapshot 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Snapshot 7 Web Access 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access. WebLink 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink 7\Web Files\License Workflow Suite 7 C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WorkflowSuite\ServerAutomation C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WorkflowSuite\WorkDistributionManager Note: On 64-bit Windows, 32-bit applications are installed in C:\Program Files (x86). Note: The Laserfiche Scanning license is usually part of the Client license file when shipped. During the installation, the Laserfiche Scanning portion of the license is copied to the Laserfiche Scanning installation path. Note: Laserfiche Audit Trail, E-mail, and Records Management do not require separate license files. They are features in the Laserfiche Server license file. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "IP2: Unhandled Exception in CDIBFilterImpl::RunEx" error occurs when using Barcode 9.1 in Quick Fields on Windows 10 5 years ago An "IP2: Unhandled Exception in CDIBFilterImpl::RunEx" error occurs when using Barcode 9.1 in Quick Fields on Windows 10 October 13, 2020 | KB: 1013781 https://support.laserfiche.com/... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013781/an-ip2-unhandled-exception ...
rows.title: An "IP2: Unhandled Exception in CDIBFilterImpl::RunEx" error occurs when using Barcode 9.1 in Quick Fields on Windows 10
rows.detailTitle: An "IP2: Unhandled Exception in CDIBFilterImpl::RunEx" error occurs when using Barcode 9.1 in Quick Fields on Windows 10
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "IP2: Unhandled Exception in CDIBFilterImpl::RunEx" error occurs when using Barcode 9.1 in Quick Fields on Windows 10 Product: Quick Fields 10 KB: 1013781 Published: June 10, 2016 Last Updated: October 13, 2020 Summary When running a Barcode 9.1 process in Quick Fields installed on a Windows 10 machine, you may receive the following error: IP2: Unhandled Exception in CDIBFilterImpl::RunEx Cause Barcode 9.1 is not supported on Windows 10. A new Quick Fields 10 install does not replace an existing Barcode 9.1 process with the new Barcode reader included with Quick Fields 10. Resolution Uninstall Barcode 9.1 before or after installing Quick Fields 10. Once uninstalled, Quick Fields 10 will use the new Barcode reader and the Barcode process will run successfully on Windows 10. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

(Exception from HRESULT: 0x800706BA)" Error Occurs when Accessing Forms. October 17, 2016 | KB: 1013804 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013804/a-q... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013804/a-the-rpc-server-is-unavailable-< ...
rows.title: A "The RPC server is unavailable. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x800706BA)" Error Occurs when Accessing Forms.
rows.detailTitle: A "The RPC server is unavailable. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x800706BA)" Error Occurs when Accessing Forms.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "The RPC server is unavailable. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x800706BA)" Error Occurs when Accessing Forms. Product: Forms 9, Forms 10 KB: 1013804 Published: October 17, 2016 Last Updated: October 17, 2016 Summary When signing in to Laserfiche Forms, you may receive the following error message: Laserfiche Forms has encountered a problem. The RPC server is unavailable. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x800706BA) Resolution This error can occur when Forms is configured to retrieve user information from a Laserfiche repository, but is unable to connect to the repository. Open the Laserfiche Forms Configuration page and review the options set on the User Authentication page. Open the Forms Configuration page through the Start menu or by typing the URL in your browser address bar. The default URL for the Configuration page is http://machinename/FormsConfig, where machinename is the name of the server where Forms is installed. Click the User Authentication tab. Verify that the Laserfiche Server, Repository, User name and Password values are valid. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for the September 6, 2024 Laserfiche App
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for the September 6, 2024 Laserfiche App Product: Laserfiche App KB: 1014541 Published: September 6, 2024 Last Updated: September 6, 2024 Summary This September 6, 2024 app release includes the following updates: iOS Resolved an issue where certain electronic documents would become blurry or degrade in quality after being zoomed in repeatedly. (538067) Resolved an issue where Mobile Server addresses with custom (non-443) port numbers failed the SSL/TLS handshake, leading to a TrustFailure (Authentication Failed; see inner exception) error message. (534400) iOS and Android Selecting "Update Related Lookup Tables" from the offline forms menu now ensures that all offline lookup tables associated with that form are updated. (537728) App Version iOS: 2024.09.04.1525891 Android: 2024.09.04.1526309 Resolution To update the Laserfiche app: Re-download the app from Google Play or the Apple App Store. Related Links 1014230 Laserfiche App Releases Laserfiche Android App 2024.09.04.1526309 APK File Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 Product: Laserfiche Forms 11 KB: 1014496 Published: December 19, 2023 Last Updated: February 6, 2025 Summary This article details each change for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 (11.0.2311.50553). To obtain Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5, download the updated Laserfiche 11 package. General Forms Updates Added support for system-level repository profiles. Created the Forms Multi-Server Configuration Utility application to make it easier to configure Forms in a DMZ environment. Refer to Laserfiche Forms Multi-Server Configuration Utility for complete details. The German language is now fully supported. Enhanced the Save to Repository event in the following ways: The entry ID created by a STR event is now included in the process variables. You can now use the same settings as the form itself for all attachments rather than having to configure each attachment. Instance history now includes the name and folder of entries saved. Additional improvements: Advanced configuration options for Forms Auditing. Added the ability to update variable values in tables and collections on in-progress instances. Enabled enforcement of unique variable names within a process. Made major improvements to the usability of the Forms debug log to help with troubleshooting. Form Designer Updates Added the ability to filter tables and collections to only show relevant data. Added basic field masking to single line fields. Extended the LFForm object for a more complete JavaScript DOM: Auxiliary UI elements can now be accessed and updated. More UI settings can be updated. Additional simple form field properties have been added to the LFForm object. Refer to JavaScript in the Forms Designer (laserfiche.com) for complete details. You can now preview a form at different steps within the process to see how rules affect what is displayed. Checkbox and radio button fields now convert to dropdown menus when the number of options exceed a set limit. Additional features: Enhanced the Custom HTML field to support JavaScript in a sandboxed environment. Note: Use of keywords such as: "document", "window", "this", "globalThis", "$", and other unsupported code will be blocked and will display errors in the developer console. Introduced the capability to set a total file size limit that encompasses all files within a file upload field. Enabled page-by-page input validation. Added the ability to detect if a form has been modified by another user when saving changes. Added support for page breaks at each page defined in the form's pagination settings when saving the form. Added the ability to search for fields on a form based on variable name, label, or field type. Fixes Forms General Fixes Extra file attachments are no longer deleted when deleting other rows in a table. (494692) AuthorizeNet.dll is now included when upgrading to Forms 11 Update 5. (478928) You no longer receive a "Timeout expired. The timeout period elapsed prior to obtaining a connection from the pool. This may have occurred because all pooled connections were in use and max pool size was reached." error message when signing in with Laserfiche Directory Server authentication. (490093) SQL deadlocks should no longer occur when multiple Workflow service tasks are run in parallel. (492963) Formulas now work properly on forms accessed from a mobile web browser on iOS devices. (474144) Forms Designer Fixes Forms now automatically scroll to the top of the next page when the next page button is clicked. (467244) Download Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 is available as part of the Laserfiche 11 package. Related Links Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3 Product: Forms 11 KB: 1014413 Published: December 20, 2022 Last Updated: July 15, 2024 Summary This article details each change for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3 (11.0.2212.30907). To obtain Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3, download the updated Laserfiche 11 package. Updates Inclusion of Laserfiche Audit Trail Starter Edition Forms Audit Trail records events that take place in Laserfiche Forms, enabling you to view those events as part of a report. Laserfiche Forms sends the audit events to Laserfiche Audit Trail for archiving and reporting. The Starter edition of Forms Audit Trail will be available for Laserfiche Forms Server with Forms Professional license and will not be available for Laserfiche Forms Server with Forms Essentials license Security Enhancements Added support for OAuth 2.0 for SMTP connections to Microsoft Office 365 and Gmail mail servers for email notifications and approvals. Added several features to increase security of Forms processing including Cross-Site Request Forgery prevention and several more secure HTTP header options. These options will be enabled by default on new installations. Task Approval Enhancements Supports direct approval via email buttons. Allows for more action buttons on user tasks. Supports the use of user task properties as variable values in user task reminder emails and other steps. Administration Updates Ability to reopen a completed/terminated instance in the main process. An admin may restart a user task in the main process with all current process variable data if an instance has any issues. Restarting tasks within a sub process is not supported. Made it easier to troubleshoot problem instances by adding the ability to filter the monitor for instances that have an error log, adding a direct link to the error log on the instance details page, and including an error log in notification emails. Process administrators can now update file upload variables for in-progress instances. Added the ability to filter by and remove inactive users or groups in bulk. Performance Improvements Improved the monitor page performance by defaulting to display the last 30 days of instances. Improved the task list performance by reducing the number of lines retrieved at a time. Improved the search performance for searching within the inbox task list. Enhanced the search performance on the field rules page. Enhanced the search performance on custom reports. Usability Updates Dropdown controls for configuring rules are now searchable and you can type any value for condition when use dropdown field in field rules and reports filters. Support time formats without seconds for both the form designer and classic form designer. Public draft options now have the option to request email confirmation from the user via a dialog. Added the ability to update an email address on public drafts. You can now update process settings when creating a process from the solution templates. When saving PDF files to repository, Forms will follow all the sub options for generate Laserfiche pages. Updates to the Form Designer When creating a new form, the form designer is now the default option rather than the classic designer. A variety of enhancements to field rules have been made, including complex AND/OR rule conditions, the ability to control more field types, and additional rule triggering events. Support showing and hiding action buttons using field rules. Support for complex field rule conditions (using AND and OR structures.) Support enabling and disabling fields with field rules. Support for setting fields to required or not required states. Support showing and hiding the next and previous page buttons. Support use field rules with conditions based on field comparisons. Support showing and hiding fields based on the stage or step of the process. Support changing validation requirements dynamically depending on conditions. Support for enabling and disabling field rules for troubleshooting. Added support for JavaScript. For more information, see online help for JavaScript in the Form Designer. Added pagination for tables and collections Added a preview option for a detailed view of images uploaded to a form. Added the ability to apply CSS classes to multiple fields at once. Support the configuration of the display size of the signature field. Added several features that existed in the classic designer: Auto suggestion in single line fields. Accept barcode scans from the Laserfiche app for single line fields and number fields. Append choices to lookup results for dropdown fields. A loading indicator when performing lookups. Deletion confirmation when removing collection sets or table rows. Customize row labels for tables with fixed rows. Support uploaded images in the custom HTML field. Use variables for number/currency/date/time field default values. Limit date/time selections in the date/time pickers based on range settin

rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5
rows.detailTitle: Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5 Product: Laserfiche Forms 11 KB: 1014497 Published: December 19, 2023 Last Updated: August 29, 2024 Summary Laserfiche introduces various new features with Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 5. For a more detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1014496. Laserfiche Forms Server System Requirements CPU: 4-core or more CPU Memory: 4 GB RAM (8 GB recommended) Operating system: Windows 10 Windows 11 Windows Server 2016 Windows Server 2019 Windows Server 2022 .NET Framework 4.8 must be installed. Note: Hardware requirements may fluctuate based on the number of users logged in to the server. If you expect to have many simultaneous connections to your Laserfiche Forms server, we encourage you to configure it with a faster CPU and/or add more RAM. Note: Server operating systems (Windows Server 2019, etc.) are preferred. Database Server Microsoft SQL Server 2014 SP3 or above Microsoft SQL Server 2016 SP2 or above Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Microsoft SQL Server 2019 Microsoft SQL Server 2022 Laserfiche Forms Software Requirements Laserfiche Server Laserfiche Forms requires Laserfiche Server (version 9 or later) or Laserfiche Directory Server (version 10.4 or later). Forms is not compatible with standalone editions of Laserfiche (Executive, Desktop, etc.). In order to integrate with Laserfiche Audit trail with Advanced features and support more event classes and failure audit, Laserfiche Forms requires Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Update 5 (or later) and Laserfiche Full Text Search 11.0 (or later). Web Server Internet Information Services (IIS): IIS 10 (Windows 10, Windows 11, Windows Server 2016, Windows Server 2019, Windows Server 2022) ASP.NET 4.x must be enabled. Client Workstation Users can fill out forms that start a process in Chrome (latest version), Safari(Mac only), Firefox (latest version), Edge (Chromium based), Opera. The Laserfiche Forms Inbox, Form Designer, Process Designer, Administrative pages, etc. must be viewed in Edge (Chromium based), Firefox (latest version), or Chrome (latest version). Laserfiche Forms also supports mobile browsing (Chrome, Firefox, and Safari recommended). Known Issues When using the Forms Server Configuration Tool, certificates need to be issued to the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the intended Forms server, and may not include a wildcard (asterisk, or "*") in the domain specification. (506849) Form designer: The row count of a collection/table will not be correct if the 'append sets' choice is enabled when a lookup rule returns less data than the previous selection and there is another field with a value inside the collection/table. (324476) Forms calculate SUM(SINGLE_LINE) formulas for read-only number fields, which are not calculated post-submission. (428035) After updating chromium to 118.0.5993.0, submitted classic forms render incorrectly when table fields have long values. (473229) Boundary Timer Catch Events can cause instances to terminate if an NLB routing service cluster has been implemented. (483428) Importing and overwriting a process may fail with an LFF502 error when both contain a task reminder with the same ID attached to a step with different ID's. (487379) Laserfiche client applications may only display a single point on the map integration when viewing the files saved in the repository even if multiple map points were specified in the Save to Repository activity in Forms. (418432) Put the previous/next button for pagination under the payment/reCAPTCHA section (368245) Improve accessibility for date picker used when fill form (370821) Cannot open instance detail page if the instance has more than 10k worker instances (395346) Forms Team JS Filter variable declaration error when test the filter/run the filter multiple times (397025) Need a way to clear the field value submitted in previous step and the field is hidden by field rule in later steps (403080) Update "Manage Access" of report schedule will trigger new snapshot generation if scheduled time is in the past. (306739) When importing a form from a current version of Forms to a previous version of Forms, the import process may not import features present in the current version of Forms. Forms only checks a user's group membership when creating their session token upon signing in. This means that removing a user from a group may not be reflected in a change in a user's Forms security until after the user signs out and signs back in. (168505) You may receive an InvalidDataSent error when you submit a form after waiting more than thirty minutes, while signed in as a Directory Server user. (134714) In certain situations, Forms may not properly process a field rule to ignore a value when payment processing is turned on. (125410) Forms may not fully synchronize some users that are members of a Directory Server group when using Laserfiche Server authentication. (74678) When dragging a variable generated by a formul
